message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1310,10 +1310,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","title":"A4.1: define metric dictionary and collection sources","description":"## Goal
define metric dictionary and collection sources

## Checklist
1. Name every scorecard metric and its exact collection source.
2. Keep metric identifiers stable enough for automation.
3. Align the dictionary with the artifact manifest contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Name every scorecard metric and its exact collection source.
2. Keep metric identifiers stable enough for automation.
3. Align the dictionary with the artifact manifest contract.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The metric dictionary names every scorecard metric used by the program, gives each metric a stable identifier, unit, aggregation rule, and exact collection source or derivation path.
2. The dictionary explicitly distinguishes cross-mode comparable metrics from mode-specific, topology-sensitive, or advisory-only metrics so later reporting does not overclaim apples-to-oranges comparisons.
3. The dictionary defines zero, missing, suppressed, and not-applicable semantics for each metric family so automation and humans interpret blanks and zeros the same way.
4. The result is reusable by G1, G5, G6, I12, and J10 without any handwritten translation layer.","notes":"Metric dictionary obligations:
- define stable metric IDs and sources for throughput, latency, retry, abort, hold/wait, split-path, page-touch, allocator, cache-hit, copy/allocation, and CPU-efficiency signals
- include units, aggregation windows, comparable-vs-mode-specific flags, and NA semantics
- require enough precision that cross-mode scorecards and verification bundles can compare runs mechanically
- optimize for future users by making the dictionary readable enough that an operator can tell what each metric means without reading code or old benchmark notes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:14:09.458459084Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:30:20.401987542Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:30:20.184167793Z","close_reason":"Metric dictionary, collection sources, comparability semantics, and artifact-contract alignment are now machine-readable and verified in the canonical harness contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","measurement","scorecard","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2360,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-15 claimed after closing bd-db300.1.3. Starting the live implementation slice now. Focus: define a machine-readable metric dictionary anchored to the existing hot-path/profile/report outputs, with stable metric IDs, units, aggregation rules, comparability flags, and explicit zero/missing/NA semantics so G1/G5/G6/J10 can reuse it without translation.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:03:27Z"},{"id":2366,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed A4.1. Added a machine-readable scorecard metric dictionary to the canonical leapfrog contract and verifier surfaces with stable metric IDs, metric families, comparability classes, zero/missing/suppressed/NA semantics, and exact templated collection-artifact paths aligned to verification_plan.logging_artifacts. Tightened validation so campaign.required_modes must exactly match sqlite_reference/fsqlite_mvcc/fsqlite_single_writer, every required_log_fields entry must resolve to a metric definition, and required claim metrics must point at declared artifacts. Verification: bash -n scripts/verify_bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria.sh; rch cargo check -p fsqlite-harness --all-targets; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria -- --nocapture (4/4 passed); full verifier gate passed and emitted artifacts under artifacts/bd-db300.7.3/bd-db300.7.3-20260315T062630Z-7300/.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:30:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","title":"A4.2: define transferability and placement-stability rubric","description":"## Goal
define transferability and placement-stability rubric

## Checklist
1. Define what counts as transferable, profile-specific, and suspicious wins.
2. Apply the rubric across modes and hardware classes.
3. Feed the rubric directly into G3 and final reporting.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Define what counts as transferable, profile-specific, and suspicious wins.
2. Apply the rubric across modes and hardware classes.
3. Feed the rubric directly into G3 and final reporting.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The rubric classifies results as transferable, profile-specific-but-useful, suspicious, or non-claimable, with concrete examples drawn from baseline, pinned, and adversarial placement profiles.
2. The rubric explains how to evaluate wins and regressions across SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer modes without hiding catastrophic losses behind a narrow local improvement.
3. The rubric defines how final reporting must label lab-specific wins, topology-sensitive wins, and no-catastrophic-regression failures.
4. The result feeds directly into G3 and G4 and is usable by future agents without reopening planning documents.","notes":"Transferability rubric obligations:
- define what counts as broadly transferable, profile-sensitive-but-still-valuable, suspicious, and non-claimable performance movement
- include a no-catastrophic-regression rule so a narrow c8 win cannot mask severe damage to c1, mixed, or adversarial-profile rows
- require user-facing claim guidance: say when the project may honestly claim parity or superiority and when it must describe a result as hardware- or profile-specific
- optimize for operator trust by making the rubric strong enough to prevent cherry-picking while still allowing useful specialized wins to be documented honestly","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:14:22.737609996Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:59:19.597051731Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:59:19.414076239Z","close_reason":"Completed: machine-readable transferability rubric added to canonical G3 scorecard contract with validation, focused test coverage, and verifier replay.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","quality-gate","scorecard","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2367,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","author":"Codex","text":"Claimed after closing bd-db300.1.4.1 based on bv --robot-triage --label db300-track-a. Starting the transferability and placement-stability rubric in the same canonical harness contract surface so scorecard thresholds, placement stability semantics, and verification artifacts stay machine-readable and downstream-reusable.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:31:57Z"},{"id":2370,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continuing after fresh bv robot triage. This remains the best concrete unblocker for Track A -> A4, which in turn blocks bd-db300.5.1 and downstream Track E design work. Starting rubric wiring in the canonical leapfrog/scorecard contract surface so transferability, topology sensitivity, and claim-label rules become machine-readable instead of prose-only.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:46:18Z"},{"id":2371,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Completed machine-readable transferability and placement-stability rubric wiring in the canonical leapfrog/scorecard contract surface. Added the transferability_rubric schema to leapfrog_exit_criteria.toml plus Rust validation, focused harness coverage, and verifier preflight so G3/G4 can consume exact classes, report labels, hardware-evidence tiers, and no-catastrophic-regression semantics without reopening planning prose. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/fsqlite-harness/src/leapfrog_exit_criteria.rs crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria.rs; bash -n scripts/verify_bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria.sh; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_db300_142 cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_db300_142 cargo check -p fsqlite-harness --all-targets; bash scripts/verify_bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria.sh.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:59:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.4.3","title":"A4.3: define c1 c4 c8 thresholds and target directions","description":"## Goal
define c1 c4 c8 thresholds and target directions

## Checklist
1. Define explicit thresholds or target directions for c1, c4, and c8.
2. Include throughput, latency, retries, and blocking indicators.
3. Keep the thresholds ready for G3 and G4.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Define explicit thresholds or target directions for c1, c4, and c8.
2. Include throughput, latency, retries, and blocking indicators.
3. Keep the thresholds ready for G3 and G4.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The scorecard defines explicit target directions and anti-regression floors for c1, c4, and c8 across throughput, tail latency, retry pressure, hold or wait indicators, and CPU-efficiency metrics where relevant.
2. The thresholds distinguish must-not-regress floors from aspirational frontier targets so implementation work can be judged honestly during partial progress.
3. The thresholds are interpretable per workload family, including insert-heavy, hot-page, and mixed rows, rather than pretending one numeric rule fits every geometry.
4. The result is directly consumable by steering, regression gating, and final scorecard work.","notes":"AmberHorizon: completed threshold-contract lane. Fixed leapfrog_exit_criteria REQUIRED_LOG_FIELDS length (8 -> 9) and workload-family ownership mismatch; fixed unrelated shared-worktree blockers in commit_repair/connection/task lifecycle and harness runtime so verification could complete. Verified with rch remote cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, targeted remote fsqlite-core write_coordinator and commit_repair tests, remote fsqlite-harness commit_repair_async, and remote bd_db300_7_3_leapfrog_exit_criteria; cargo fmt --check passed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:14:35.880887150Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T22:39:55.957517080Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T22:39:55.956861682Z","close_reason":"Completed with remote verification: workspace clippy clean; targeted db300 and commit-repair tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-a","scorecard","thresholds","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.1.5","title":"A5: classify hot cells by dominant bound type (sync, CPU, cache/memory, or I/O)","description":"## Goal
Classify each canonical hot benchmark cell by dominant bound type and microarchitectural signature so the optimization program stops conflating lock contention, cache-line ping-pong, remote NUMA access, TLB or branch waste, allocator churn, and durable I/O. This bead is the explicit answer to the question "what is spending cycles and wall time on 32-64 core machines?" rather than a generic hotspot label.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each canonical hot cell is classified by dominant bound type with supporting counters and trace evidence.
- The output decomposes `queueing + synchronization + retries + service + allocator/cache + durable I/O` and records the dominant microarchitectural signature (`HITM/false-sharing`, `remote NUMA`, `LLC/TLB miss`, `branch/uop`, or `syscall/durability`).
- Ambiguous or mixed-bound cells are called out explicitly with confidence notes instead of forced into a false category.
- The resulting classification is detailed enough to steer share-nothing lane design, false-sharing elimination, residual-lock strategy, and durability-lane work.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"- Each canonical hot cell is classified by dominant bound type with supporting counters and trace evidence.
- The output decomposes `queueing + synchronization + retries + service + allocator/cache + durable I/O` and records the dominant microarchitectural signature (`HITM/false-sharing`, `remote NUMA`, `LLC/TLB miss`, `branch/uop`, or `syscall/durability`).
- Ambiguous or mixed-bound cells are called out explicitly with confidence notes instead of forced into a false category.
- The resulting classification is detailed enough to steer share-nothing lane design, false-sharing elimination, residual-lock strategy, and durability-lane work.
- The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"The key deliverable is a table or artifact pack that ties specific workloads and fixtures to dominant bottlenecks such as futex-heavy synchronization, allocator/copy CPU cost, cache/memory pressure, or durable-write/I/O limits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:39:14.769160467Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:55:29.346022637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","perf","profiling","statistics","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.5.1","title":"A5.1: decompose hot cells into queue sync service allocator and durability time","description":"## Goal
decompose hot cells into queue sync service allocator and durability time

## Checklist
1. Break hot-cell wall time into queueing, synchronization, retry, service, allocator or copy, and durability components.
2. Base the decomposition on captured evidence rather than estimates.
3. Make the result ready for optimization steering.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Break hot-cell wall time into queueing, synchronization, retry, service, allocator or copy, and durability components.

Starting with code archaeology on the hot-path report/summary pipeline to identify where wall time can be decomposed into queueing, synchronization, retry, service, allocator-copy, and durability components from captured evidence rather than estimates.","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:59:53Z"},{"id":2351,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.1","author":"Codex","text":"Implemented A5.1 slice: added runtime-phase timing evidence to EngineRunReport, wired executor capture for queueing/synchronization/retry/service/rollback/commit timings, added evidence-backed wall_time_components to hot-path actionable rankings and markdown/script aggregation, and verified with cargo fmt --check, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, and targeted rch tests. Targeted clippy on -p fsqlite-e2e is currently blocked by unrelated clippy::significant-drop-in-scrutinee in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs under another agent lane.","created_at":"2026-03-15T04:33:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","title":"A5.2: tag hot cells with microarchitectural signatures","description":"## Goal
tag hot cells with microarchitectural signatures

## Checklist
1. Tag each hot cell with the dominant signature such as HITM, remote NUMA, LLC or TLB pressure, branch waste, or durability pressure.
2. Mark mixed or ambiguous cases honestly.
3. Keep the tags usable by architecture and tuning work.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Tag each hot cell with the dominant signature such as HITM, remote NUMA, LLC or TLB pressure, branch waste, or durability pressure.
2. Mark mixed or ambiguous cases honestly.
3. Keep the tags usable by architecture and tuning work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Each hot cell is tagged with one or more dominant microarchitectural signatures such as HITM/ownership ping-pong, remote NUMA traffic, LLC pressure, TLB pressure, branch waste, front-end starvation, or durability pressure, with mixed cases marked honestly.
2. The tags are tied back to canonical matrix rows, hardware signature, placement profile, and the specific counters or traces that justified them.
3. The output is directly usable by architecture, tuning, and prioritization work without reopening raw perf-counter dumps.
4. Ambiguous or mixed cases remain explicit instead of being forced into a false single-label story.","notes":"Classification obligations:
- translate raw topology-aware counter and trace data into a compact microarchitectural signature map for each hot cell
- require provenance back to the specific row, hardware profile, placement profile, and capture source that justified the tag
- preserve mixed/ambiguous classifications so later architecture work can see when a row is multi-bound instead of pretending one culprit explains everything
- optimize for users by turning counter archaeology into a reusable steering artifact rather than a one-off expert memory","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:02.629775278Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-16T23:13:47.666383025Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T23:13:47.665718091Z","close_reason":"Status-hygiene close: microarchitectural signature tagging is landed in the current tree and evidenced by actionable_ranking.microarchitectural_signatures, mixed/ambiguous classification handling, and reproducible reporting outputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","statistics","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2355,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting A5.2 immediately after closing A5.1. Choosing this as the highest-impact non-overlapping follow-on from the freshly landed wall-time decomposition, while global bv still ranks parent placeholder bd-db300.1.3 and UmberHawk remains active on bd-db300.1.3.3. Beginning archaeology on the hot-path classification/counter provenance pipeline to add honest microarchitectural signature tagging with explicit mixed-case handling.","created_at":"2026-03-15T05:32:56Z"},{"id":2361,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","author":"Codex","text":"Implemented A5.2 microarchitectural signature tagging across hot-path artifacts. Actionable ranking now emits evidence-backed signature entries with explicit mixed/ambiguous handling plus canonical row/fixture/mode/placement/hardware context; realdb-e2e resolves baseline canonical matrix context from the checked-in campaign manifest; DB300 verification script aggregates and summarizes the new signature lane. Verified with rch cargo check on realdb-e2e, targeted inline-bundle/artifact tests, and bash -n on the script. Clippy full-crate verification is still noisy from unrelated crate hygiene and was being chased down via small fixture_select/fsqlite_executor cleanups.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:05:27Z"},{"id":2409,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.5.2","author":"codex","text":"2026-03-16 Codex audit: closing after status-hygiene review. The A5.2 deliverable is present in the current tree and checked-in artifacts: hot-path actionable ranking emits evidence-backed microarchitectural_signatures with explicit mixed_or_ambiguous handling; scripts/verify_bd_db300_4_1_hot_path_profiles.sh validates and summarizes that signature lane; and the representative hot-cell plus J1 truthing artifact packs consume the new fields directly. Prior bead comments already recorded the targeted implementation-slice verification.","created_at":"2026-03-16T23:13:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.1.6","title":"A6: capture hardware topology, affinity, NUMA/SMT state, and placement policy","description":"## Goal
Capture the hardware-topology and placement contract for this optimization campaign so results can be interpreted correctly on high-end Threadripper and Xeon-class machines. This includes socket, node, CCD/CCX/LLC topology where discoverable, SMT sibling relationships, preferred-core and boost behavior, memory topology, pinning policy, memory-placement policy, and any runtime environment details that materially affect write-path scaling.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Artifact bundles record CPU model, stepping, and microcode or firmware revision where accessible; socket or NUMA topology; CCD/CCX/LLC domains where discoverable; SMT state; memory topology or NUMA distances; kernel version; governor or EPP/energy-policy state; boost/C-state state; huge-page or THP state; relevant scheduler or IRQ placement settings; and the benchmark placement profile.
2. The benchmark methodology states when pinning, SMT spreading or packing, NUMA-local allocation, interleave, automatic NUMA balancing, IRQ pinning, compile/helper-lane placement, or other placement strategies are used or intentionally avoided.
3. Future many-core measurements can be reproduced on comparable hardware without rediscovering the environment contract.
4. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Artifact bundles record CPU model, stepping, and microcode or firmware revision where accessible; socket or NUMA topology; CCD/CCX/LLC domains where discoverable; SMT state; memory topology or NUMA distances; kernel version; governor or EPP/energy-policy state; boost/C-state state; huge-page or THP state; relevant scheduler or IRQ placement settings; and the benchmark placement profile.
2. The benchmark methodology states when pinning, SMT spreading or packing, NUMA-local allocation, interleave, automatic NUMA balancing, IRQ pinning, compile/helper-lane placement, or other placement strategies are used or intentionally avoided.
3. Future many-core measurements can be reproduced on comparable hardware without rediscovering the environment contract.
4. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead exists because 32+ core machines can mislead badly when affinity, NUMA placement, SMT sibling use, or boost policy are left implicit. The environment contract must be explicit before the project can claim many-core insights confidently.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:39:26.354619971Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:58:57.067739966Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:58:57.067181390Z","close_reason":"Completed topology, placement contract, and side-condition capture work via A6.1/A6.2/A6.3","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","mechanical-sympathy","parallelism","perf","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2377,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"bv --robot-triage --label db300-track-a surfaced A6 as the current highest-impact Track A item. Continuing implementation through active child bd-db300.1.6.3, focused on IRQ/helper-lane and NUMA memory-policy side-condition capture so the parent contract reflects live work.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:49:49Z"},{"id":2382,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Track-A triage still ranks A6 as the top actionable slice. Continuing via active child bd-db300.1.6.3; next code step is the artifact-persistence hook so the new side_conditions bundle is discoverable from the shared artifact manifest instead of only by convention.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:44:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","title":"A6.1: capture topology firmware and power-state discovery bundle","description":"## Goal
capture topology firmware and power-state discovery bundle

## Checklist
1. Capture CPU model, firmware or microcode where possible, socket or node layout, SMT relationships, boost or EPP state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. Tie the bundle to benchmark sessions.
3. Keep the capture format reusable by manifests and reports.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Capture CPU model, firmware or microcode where possible, socket or node layout, SMT relationships, boost or EPP state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. Tie the bundle to benchmark sessions.
3. Keep the capture format reusable by manifests and reports.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The discovery bundle records the canonical hardware signature fields needed for run identity, including CPU model/stepping, microcode or firmware where available, socket/NUMA/LLC topology, SMT relationships, boost or governor state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings.
2. The bundle is tied mechanically to benchmark sessions and artifact manifests using the stable naming/provenance contract, so future runs do not invent ad hoc hardware IDs or paths.
3. The result is directly reusable by A3.3 manifests, G6.1 run-identity logging, and operator-facing reports without translation.
4. The bead makes it obvious when hardware identity is incomplete or partially unavailable instead of silently pretending the environment is fully characterized.","notes":"Discovery-bundle obligations:
- define the canonical hardware signature fields that become part of run identity, including CPU model/stepping, microcode or firmware where available, socket or NUMA layout, LLC domains, SMT relationships, boost/governor state, THP state, and relevant scheduler settings
- bind the discovery bundle to the stable artifact naming/provenance contract so hardware identity is mechanically attached to runs rather than stored as an informal side note
- make partial discovery explicit, including which fields are unavailable, inferred, or intentionally omitted
- optimize for users by making environment drift and machine-to-machine mismatch visible before benchmark results are interpreted too confidently","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackHill","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:29.954161942Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:34:15.989945215Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T06:34:03.240401770Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","evidence","measurement","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:10.693021565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2364,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","author":"BlackHill","text":"Claiming this bead on a low-conflict surface. Planned edits: new script `scripts/verify_bd_db300_1_6_1_topology_bundle.sh` plus clean doc update in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md`. Scope is the reusable topology/firmware/power-state discovery bundle tied to benchmark run identity and artifact provenance, avoiding the already-dirty runtime/e2e implementation files.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:26:09Z"},{"id":2368,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","author":"BlackHill","text":"Verification update: added `scripts/verify_bd_db300_1_6_1_topology_bundle.sh` plus operator docs in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/RUNNING_THE_E2E_SUITE.md`. The script now emits `hardware_discovery_bundle.json` and `hardware_discovery_summary.md`, computes a stable `hardware_signature`, captures CPU/microcode/BIOS topology cpufreq THP and scheduler state, and records explicit missing/inferred fields. Verified with `bash -n scripts/verify_bd_db300_1_6_1_topology_bundle.sh`, a temp-directory run using explicit run-id/placement/hardware-class fields, and a manifest-linked run confirming the `manifest_snapshot` contract and zero mismatch notes when the manifest and env agree.","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:33:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","title":"A6.2: define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles","description":"## Goal
define execution contract for baseline pinned and adversarial placement profiles

## Checklist
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Define exact execution rules for baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial profiles.
2. Spell out pinning, SMT policy, memory policy, and helper-lane placement.
3. Make the contract ready for G7 and Track H reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The execution contract defines baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles with exact rules for CPU pinning, SMT packing or spreading, NUMA or memory policy, helper-lane placement, and any required environment disclosures.
2. Each profile includes a user-facing invocation contract showing how suites and focused reruns select it, which knobs are fixed versus optional, and when the profile is mandatory for a claim.
3. The contract is stable enough that G7 suites, Track H comparison work, and future operators can reproduce placement-sensitive runs without hidden tribal knowledge.
4. The result makes it clear when a run violated the placement contract and therefore cannot be compared cleanly.","notes":"Execution-contract obligations:
- define baseline, recommended pinned, and adversarial placement profiles concretely enough that humans and agents can invoke them without guesswork
- specify CPU pinning, SMT policy, memory placement, helper-lane placement, and required environment disclosures for each profile
- align profile naming and run identity with the stable artifact naming/provenance contract so operators can predict artifact locations and compare like with like
- include operator guidance on when each profile is required, optional, or intentionally avoided
- optimize for users by preventing false comparisons caused by silent drift in pinning or memory policy between runs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:15:44.131929556Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T07:47:40.237157184Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T07:26:25.061417210Z","close_reason":"Completed: exact baseline/recommended/adversarial placement execution contract added to the live campaign manifest/types/docs with targeted verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","db300-program","db300-track-a","parallelism","perf","track-a"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:22.293473399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:25:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2372,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed after closing bd-db300.1.4.2 via fresh bv robot triage. Starting the execution-contract slice by reading the existing topology discovery bundle, placement-profile taxonomy, fixture selection surfaces, and artifact naming contract so baseline/recommended/adversarial profile rules land in the canonical reusable surface instead of scattered notes.","created_at":"2026-03-15T07:00:13Z"},{"id":2373,"issue_id":"bd-db300.1.6.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Completed the placement execution contract in the live benchmark campaign surface. Also fixed an unrelated clippy lock-scope issue in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs uncovered during verification.
